beats.activity: Wake up with coffee and spin a record—Coltrane, naturally.
beats.reasoning: Early riser habit; Andre needs his morning ritual before a transit-strike day. 30 min covers coffee, vinyl, and mental prep.

beats.activity: Trail run through Discovery Park—quiet morning, clear head before the day gets complicated.
beats.reasoning: Part of Andre's daily rhythm; mild weather is perfect. Discovery Park is his go-to and avoids the transit chaos by staying local.

beats.activity: Bike commute from Magnolia to Fremont—familiar route, no transit stress.
beats.reasoning: Metro strike until 4pm means rideshare is surged; Andre's a bike mechanic—he'll pedal. Magnolia to Fremont is ~6 km, ~30 min by bike on familiar streets.

beats.activity: Work at the shop: fix drivetrain issues, adjust brakes, prep tour bikes for weekend.
beats.reasoning: Regular mechanic shift; mild weather and transit strike won't affect the shop's flow. Tour bikes need prepping—consistent with his weekend guide gig.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at Ballard Coffee—step outside the shop, stay nearby, watch the city adjust to the strike.
beats.reasoning: Close to Fremont, familiar spot. Mild weather means outdoor seating is pleasant. Gives him a moment to observe the transit-strike rhythm without being rushed.
Ballard to Fremont is walkable (~1.5 km); gives Andre time to decompress after lunch before the afternoon rush.

beats.activity: Afternoon shop work: consultations, repairs, help stranded commuters finding bike alternatives to the strike.
beats.reasoning: Transit strike likely brings bike-curious people to the shop. Andre's steady, curious nature means he'd help folks figure out routes and rentals—good business and good karma.

beats.activity: Bike ride home to Magnolia; transit strike is ending, but Andre sticks with his bike.
beats.reasoning: Strike ends at 4pm, but Andre's already on his bike routine. Fremont to Magnolia ~6 km; 40 min at easy pace on a mild evening.

beats.activity: Dinner on the porch with a view; sort through vinyl and sketch out next weekend's route.; Write a letter by hand, listen to records, prepare gear for the weekend tour.
beats.reasoning: Mild evening, partly cloudy—perfect porch time. Andre's routine: food, records, planning. Low energy yesterday (55/100) means he'll stay home, recharge quietly.

diary:
Started with Coltrane and coffee—the only way to begin a Thursday when the city's about to fall apart. Discovery Park was mine alone that early; the trail quiet enough to think straight. Biked to the shop through Fremont, grateful I've never relied on those buses anyway.

The strike hit different than I expected. Shop was packed with people suddenly remembering they own bicycles. Fixed drivetrain issues, adjusted brakes, mostly listened to folks panic about getting home. Grabbed lunch at Ballard Coffee and watched the chaos from the window—people standing on corners, checking their phones, faces tight. By afternoon we were helping stranded commuters cobble together alternatives. Felt useful, honestly.

Rode home as the strike officially ended at four, but I didn't notice or care. Porch dinner with the city spreading out below, then sorted through vinyl and sketched next weekend's route by hand. Something about today—the quiet morning, the afternoon purpose—settled something in me.
thought_process: I leaned into my advantage today without thinking twice about it. The transit strike would've paralyzed most people, but I've built a life around two wheels and my own pace. That gave me freedom and a strange clarity. The morning run and bike commute felt intentional rather than defiant. By lunch, watching the scramble, I realized the shop was becoming a refuge—people needed what we offered. I stayed focused on the work, helped where I could. Riding home after four, I didn't rush. The strike ending felt almost irrelevant. I was already home, already calm. Spending the evening with vinyl and hand-sketched routes felt like the natural rhythm of things. No rush, no noise. Just preparation and presence.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
